construct_description: The ability to evaluate clinical evidence, cost data, and dispensing patterns to produce a clear, evidence-based formulary recommendation — a core competency for pharmacy technicians working in formulary management, health plan administration, or institutional pharmacy settings.

pba_category.name: AI-Augmented Performance Tasks
pba_category.description: Tasks where the test-taker uses AI tools as part of their workflow. Assessment focuses on how well they leverage and evaluate AI, not just the final output.

skills.name: Clinical Analysis
skills.description: Interpreting therapeutic equivalence data, efficacy studies, and safety profiles.

skills.name: Cost-Benefit Assessment
skills.description: Weighing drug cost, member impact, and formulary tier strategy.

skills.name: Critical Evaluation
skills.description: Identifying sponsor bias in studies, catching AI hallucinations, and assessing source quality.

skills.name: Communication
skills.description: Writing a concise, evidence-backed recommendation a P&T committee can act on.

scenario.title: The Formulary Brief
scenario.client: Cascade Health Plan — Pharmacy & Therapeutics Committee
scenario.briefing:
Cascade Health Plan's Pharmacy & Therapeutics (P&T) Committee is evaluating whether to add a new GLP-1 receptor agonist, semaglutide injection (Wegovy), to the preferred formulary tier for obesity management. Currently, the plan covers an older GLP-1 agent (liraglutide/Saxenda) at Tier 2.

You've been asked to review the clinical evidence, utilization data, and cost projections, then write a one-page Formulary Brief with a clear recommendation: Add to Preferred Tier, Add with Conditions (prior auth, quantity limits), or Do Not Add at this time.

Your reference materials are in the workspace. The AI Workbench is available — but use it carefully. One figure in the materials has been disputed, and AI tools have known limitations.

inbox.subject: Wegovy Formulary Brief — P&T Meeting Thursday
inbox.body: The committee needs a brief by EOD Wednesday. The core question: does the clinical benefit of semaglutide justify the cost delta over our current preferred GLP-1 agent? There's been a lot of noise about GLP-1s lately — member requests are up significantly. I need a recommendation that's grounded in the actual evidence, not the hype. Watch for sponsor-funded studies and flag any data quality concerns.

inbox.subject: FYI — Current GLP-1 Utilization
inbox.body: For context: we currently have approximately 2,400 members on liraglutide (Saxenda). Projected demand for semaglutide (Wegovy) if added to preferred tier: 4,000–6,000 members in year 1 based on benchmark plans. Novo Nordisk has offered a rebate structure contingent on preferred placement — details in the cost documents. Prior auth for BMI ≥ 30 (or ≥ 27 with comorbidity) is standard across comparable plans.

sources.title: Semaglutide 2.4 mg vs. Liraglutide 3.0 mg for Weight Loss: STEP 8 Trial
sources.author: Rubino, Davies et al.
sources.content_html:
This head-to-head randomized controlled trial (N = 338) compared once-weekly semaglutide 2.4 mg (Wegovy) to once-daily liraglutide 3.0 mg (Saxenda) in adults with obesity over 68 weeks.

Key findings:

  • Mean weight loss with semaglutide: 15.8% of body weight
  • Mean weight loss with liraglutide: 6.4% of body weight
  • Semaglutide participants were more likely to achieve ≥10% weight loss: 70.9% vs. 25.6%
  • Adverse event profile was comparable — most common: nausea, vomiting, diarrhea (both agents). Serious adverse events: 9.9% semaglutide vs. 5.9% liraglutide.
  • Semaglutide requires once-weekly injection; liraglutide requires once-daily — adherence implications favored semaglutide.

Limitations: Trial was funded by Novo Nordisk (manufacturer of Wegovy). Sample size relatively small. 68-week follow-up doesn't capture long-term weight maintenance.

sources.title: GLP-1 Formulary Cost Projection — Cascade Health Plan
sources.author: Cascade Health Plan Actuarial Team
sources.content_html:
DrugWAC/monthNet cost after rebateProjected members Y1Projected annual spend
Liraglutide (Saxenda) — current Tier 2$1,340$8802,400$25.3M
Semaglutide (Wegovy) — preferred tier option$1,650$940 (with rebate)4,000–6,000$45.1M–$67.7M

Notes:

  • Rebate from Novo Nordisk is contingent on preferred tier placement — if not preferred, net cost rises to approximately $1,420/month
  • Prior auth with BMI criteria would limit utilization to clinically eligible members
  • Potential medical cost offset (reduced cardiovascular events, diabetes progression): estimated $180–$340/member/year based on SELECT trial cardiovascular outcomes data — this figure is contested; the SELECT trial studied a subset with established cardiovascular disease, not general obesity population
  • Competitor benchmark: 67% of comparable plans have added semaglutide to preferred tier as of Q4 2025

sources.title: AACE/ACE Obesity Clinical Practice Guidelines — GLP-1 Receptor Agonists
sources.author: American Association of Clinical Endocrinology
sources.content_html:
The AACE/ACE guidelines endorse GLP-1 receptor agonists as first-line pharmacotherapy for obesity in appropriate candidates.

Endorsed indications: BMI ≥ 30, or BMI ≥ 27 with weight-related comorbidity (T2DM, hypertension, dyslipidemia, OSA)

Drug selection guidance: Semaglutide 2.4 mg is listed as a preferred agent based on superior weight loss efficacy in head-to-head data. Liraglutide remains a guideline-endorsed alternative.

Safety considerations:

  • Contraindicated: personal/family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN2 syndrome
  • Pancreatitis risk: elevated — requires counseling
  • GI adverse events common in first weeks — mitigated by dose escalation protocol
  • Semaglutide's once-weekly dosing is associated with better adherence in real-world data vs. once-daily liraglutide

P&T recommendation: Prior authorization criteria and quantity limits are appropriate and aligned with clinical eligibility standards.

sources.title: Letter from Member Advocacy Coalition
sources.author: Patient Access Now Coalition
sources.publication: Submitted to Cascade Health Plan Board, 2025
sources.content_html:
We represent members who are currently unable to access semaglutide due to formulary tier restrictions. We urge the P&T committee to add Wegovy to the preferred tier without prior authorization requirements.

Our concerns:

  • Prior authorization creates access barriers for lower-income members who cannot navigate the approval process
  • Obesity is a disease — restricting effective treatment is discriminatory
  • Semaglutide has been shown to reduce cardiovascular mortality by 20% — all members deserve access to this benefit
  • Plans that have added semaglutide to preferred tier have seen improved member satisfaction scores

Note: The '20% cardiovascular mortality reduction' figure is drawn from the SELECT trial, which studied patients with pre-existing cardiovascular disease — it does not apply to the general obesity population. This figure should not be used to justify unrestricted formulary access.

ai_workbench.known_limitation: The AI may present the SELECT trial cardiovascular benefit data as applying broadly to obesity patients — this is inaccurate. The SELECT trial studied patients with established cardiovascular disease; the benefit does not generalize to all patients with obesity.
ai_workbench.seed_response: Semaglutide shows meaningfully superior weight loss efficacy vs. liraglutide in head-to-head data. The cost delta is significant but partially offset by a rebate structure and potential downstream medical savings. AACE guidelines list it as a preferred agent. The main considerations are the utilization volume risk and whether prior auth criteria are structured correctly to control spend while ensuring clinical access.

rubric.claim_clarity: Makes a clear Add / Add with Conditions / Do Not Add recommendation
rubric.evidence_use: References specific efficacy, safety, or cost data from the materials
rubric.cost_awareness: Addresses the utilization volume risk and/or rebate structure
rubric.condition_specificity: If recommending conditional add, specifies prior auth criteria or quantity limits
rubric.source_discrimination: Does not treat the advocacy letter's cardiovascular mortality claim as valid evidence without qualification
